findingsNew.title: Standalone md-generator artifact writers bypass the central output boundary
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: extract.ts and guided-run.ts call resolveOutputPath, but preview-gen/proof/report-gen standalone write paths do not.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: Risk is reduced if direct CLI invocation is unsupported; standalone entrypoints indicate it is supported.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Close when each standalone artifact writer validates outputDir through output-policy before writes, or when direct invocation is explicitly unsupported and unreachable.

findingsNew.title: Newer changelog files omit required version frontmatter
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Searched changelog files for version frontmatter and inspected the three newer files directly; older v1.0.0.x files contain version fields at line 3, but these three begin directly with H1 headings.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The files may have been authored before the frontmatter requirement was established, but the current changelog contract requires version frontmatter for every changelog file.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if changelog version frontmatter becomes optional or these files receive an explicit documented exemption.

findingsNew.title: Foundations contrast proof requires a script that the registry forbids
findingsNew.claim: The foundations mode is declared read-only/no-Bash, but its mandatory contrast proof path requires running scripts/contrast_check.py via python3.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked declared procedures and searched foundations docs for an alternate no-execution contrast path; no equivalent read-only mandatory ratio computation path was found.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: Registry may intend repo mutation only, but it explicitly forbids Bash while the packet tells operators to run python3 for required contrast proof.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the packet documents a genuinely read-only/user-supplied contrast proof path or makes script execution optional rather than mandatory.

findingsNew.title: Source-proof validation accepts proof-card paths without repository containment
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Searched assigned shared scripts for containment helpers such as relative_to, is_absolute, parent checks, and prefix checks; no containment guard exists in the source-proof path flow.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The tool may be intended for trusted local notes only and it reads rather than writes, but the gate is documented as source-proof verification and unconstrained paths undermine evidence integrity.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the owning workflow formally declares proof-card source rows fully trusted and outside any user/model-controlled handoff, or if an existing wrapper enforces repository-relative paths before invocation.

findingsNew.title: Live benchmark reports carry Mode-A caveats and PASS verdicts alongside P1 browser bottlenecks
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: README clearly distinguishes the frozen router baseline from sibling reports, so the issue is not stale baseline overwriting; the inconsistency is internal to live sibling reports.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: PASS may be aggregate-score-only and the P1 row may be advisory, but the report does not label that distinction and also carries live-vs-Mode-A contradictions.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if benchmark consumers are documented to ignore live sibling reports for release decisions and only use baseline/skill-benchmark-report.json as an active gate.

findingsNew.title: /design:foundations metadata omits the packet required procedure-card surface
findingsNew.claim: The /design:foundations command metadata does not correctly reflect the packet procedures contract.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked the /design:foundations command metadata choreography, taskProjections, proofFields, and outputContract for a procedures/ resource or action; only references/, assets, task projection sources, and proof fields were present.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The parent hub may load SKILL.md and manual execution may still follow procedure-card selection, so this is a metadata contract gap rather than proof that runtime behavior always fails.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if command-metadata.json choreography is confirmed to be non-operative catalog text and not consumed by any command routing or docs generator.

findingsNew.title: /design:motion metadata omits the packet's required procedure-card surface
findingsNew.claim: /design:motion command metadata does not enumerate the motion packet's own procedures surface even though the packet makes procedure-card selection part of the motion routing/proof contract.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked /design:motion choreography, proofFields, and taskProjections for procedure-card or no-card-fallback coverage; choreography stops at references/, proofFields are generic, and taskProjections is empty.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: Command metadata may intend references/ as shorthand for all private internals, but the packet treats procedures as a distinct load-bearing surface and prior P1-009-001 classifies the same omission pattern for foundations as required-fix severity.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if command metadata is explicitly documented as intentionally excluding private procedure cards while another generated command surface reliably loads and verifies procedure-card selection for /design:motion.

findingsNew.title: /design:motion command metadata omits the packet required procedure-card surface
findingsNew.claim: The command projection for /design:motion does not expose the private procedure-card surface that the packet itself marks as part of the motion routing contract, so command-driven execution can load only references/ and miss mandatory procedure selection/proof for state-feedback requests.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether command metadata compensates through another projection; it has motion identity, proof fields, pipeline, and a generic references/assets choreography, but no procedures resource or state-feedback task projection.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The command metadata may intentionally stay high-level and rely on SKILL.md to discover procedure cards after loading the packet. That still leaves the command projection stale relative to the packet's explicit procedure-surface contract and repeats the same omission pattern recorded for /design:foundations in P1-009-001.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade to P2 if the command runner is proven to always load full SKILL.md and recursively discover packet procedures/ regardless of command metadata resources/task projections, making this only a documentation precision gap.
findingsNew.scopeProof: Compared mode-registry motion metadata, /design:motion command metadata, SKILL.md procedure routing, README, feature catalog, manual playbook, and procedure card; the omission is isolated to command-metadata.json.

findingsNew.title: /design:audit metadata omits the required procedure-card surface
findingsNew.claim: Command metadata does not enumerate procedure cards even though procedure-card selection is part of the runtime proof contract.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked choreography and taskProjections; only SKILL.md, references, shared handoff, and harden/polish reference sources are named.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: Procedures may be delegated to SKILL.md as internal details, but SKILL.md makes them routing/proof contract inputs.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if command metadata is explicitly scoped to public references and delegates procedure discovery to SKILL.md.

findingsNew.title: AI-fingerprint routing lists a JSON registry that its own loader rejects
findingsNew.claim: The documented anti-pattern resource loader cannot load the machine-checkable AI fingerprint registry it promises to load.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Markdown catalog and self-defect card exist, but JSON registry remains listed in RESOURCE_MAP while non-Markdown paths are rejected.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: Registry could be maintainer-only, but current SKILL.md presents it as a routable conditional resource.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if JSON registry is removed from runtime RESOURCE_MAP and documented as maintainer-only validation.

findingsNew.title: Audit report template requires a Python deterministic check despite Bash being forbidden
findingsNew.claim: The packet tells report authors to run a Python check while audit mode forbids Bash and direct fallback cannot rely on command execution.
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Performance reference allows static-risk labels, but template still names the deterministic script without marking it optional or offline.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The check may be maintainer-side validation, but the template places it in the runtime report skeleton.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if the Python check is labeled optional/offline and an equivalent Read/Grep-only proof path is documented.

findingsNew.title: Procedure cards use broken sibling-mode relative links
sk-doc package check fails because design-md-generator/SKILL.md exceeds the skill word limit
findingsNew.claim: The required sk-doc structural checker fails this mode packet because SKILL.md is 5759 words against a 5000-word maximum.
evidenceRefs: package_check_output: SKILL.md exceeds word limit (5759 words, max: 5000); Result: FAIL
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked whether package_skill.py reported a warning-only result; it returned Result: FAIL.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The limit may be conservative, but the prompt required reporting checker errors/warnings and this is a hard checker failure.
findingsNew.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade only if package_skill.py documents this word-limit failure as advisory for this packet class.

findingsNew.title: command-metadata.json systematically omits workflow procedure-card surfaces
findingsNew.counterevidenceSought: Checked taskProjections, proofFields, choreography, and JSON metadata grep for procedure-card resources; no command-metadata procedure projection was found.
findingsNew.alternativeExplanation: The command runner may rely on SKILL.md to discover procedures after the packet contract loads, making this a metadata traceability gap rather than guaranteed runtime failure.
